name: Sustainability Roadmap
summary: A portfolio view of how aerospace programmes reduce emissions through operations, materials, propulsion, energy sources, and business-model choices over time.
whenToUseIt: Use it when a team needs a credible decarbonisation path instead of a single technology bet that ignores infrastructure and certification reality.
steps: Measure the current emissions drivers across operations and design.
Separate near-term operational wins from medium-term technology shifts.
Map dependencies such as fuel availability, charging, airport infrastructure, and certification.
Prioritise initiatives by impact, readiness, and risk.
Track the roadmap as technology and policy conditions change.
example: An aviation company may find that the first meaningful gains come from drag cleanup and operational procedures, while hydrogen or battery concepts stay on the roadmap as longer-horizon bets rather than near-term promises.
